data.title: INVESTIGATION ALERT: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP Announces Investigation of Moviynt Data Breach
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / July 24, 2025 / Moviynt recently disclosed that it suffered a data breach that compromised the sensitive personal data of individuals. This data breach has led to concerns over the security of sensitive personal information entrusted to Moviynt.

WHAT'S THIS ABOUT?

Recently, Moviynt became aware of a security incident on its internal employee email system. Upon detection, Moviynt launched an investigation with the assistance of third-party cyber security experts to determine the nature and scope of the incident. The investigation determined that an unauthorized third party gained access to employee email accounts and files between February 27, 2025, and March 6, 2025. The impacted files may contain personal information of individuals, including names and Social Security numbers.

On July 8, 2025, Moviynt began sending notice letters to impacted individuals and filed a notice with the New Hampshire Attorney General's Office. Compensation may be available for those individuals who received notice that their personal information was compromised.

WHY YOU MAY BE ENTITLED TO COMPENSATION?

Data breaches are serious matters that can cause long-term damage. Hackers may use stolen information to commit identity theft, financial fraud, or other crimes. Companies that fail to secure your personal data may be held liable for the resulting harm.

HOW DO I KNOW IF I WAS AFFECTED?

If you received a data breach notification letter from Moviynt, you are likely affected. Follow the link below to find out if you may be eligible for compensation.

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP is investigating whether affected individuals are entitled to compensation. There is no cost or obligation to participate. Follow the link below to find out:

https://tinyurl.com/3pupfysa

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP is a nationally recognized consumer advocacy law firm that has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars against large corporations. The firm's team of over 70 extraordinary attorneys and professionals have a winning track record going against the most powerful defense attorneys in the world and know how to maximize your compensation. Our firm works on a 100% contingency fee basis - you pay nothing unless we win your case! Visit our website to learn more.

Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

Ed Korsinsky, Esq.

33 Whitehall Street, 17th Floor

New York, NY 10004

jlevi@levikorsinsky.com

Tel: (212) 363-7500

Fax: (212) 363-7171

https://zlk.com/

data.title: First Bank NJ Is Maintained at Buy by DA Davidson
VALERO ENERGY CORP/TX SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Valero Energy Corp, a leading international manufacturer and marketer of transportation fuels and petrochemical products, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting the challenges and opportunities faced in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $29,889 million, reflecting a decrease from $34,490 million in the same quarter of 2024 due to lower sales volumes and prices.
  • Operating Income: $997 million, down from $1,221 million in the second quarter of 2024, primarily due to decreased margins in the Renewable Diesel and Ethanol segments.
  • Net Income: $663 million, compared to $926 million in the second quarter of 2024, impacted by lower operating income and asset impairment losses.
  • Earnings per Common Share: $2.28, down from $2.71 in the second quarter of 2024, reflecting the decrease in net income.

Business Highlights

  • Refining Segment Performance: The Refining segment reported operating income of $1,266 million for the second quarter of 2025, a slight increase from $1,224 million in the same period of 2024. This segment includes operations of petroleum refineries and associated marketing activities, with principal products being gasolines, blendstocks, and distillates.
  • Renewable Diesel Segment Performance: The Renewable Diesel segment experienced an operating loss of $79 million in the second quarter of 2025, compared to an operating income of $112 million in the same period of 2024. This segment focuses on producing renewable diesel, renewable naphtha, and neat sustainable aviation fuel (SAF).
  • Ethanol Segment Performance: The Ethanol segment reported an operating income of $54 million for the second quarter of 2025, down from $105 million in the same period of 2024. This segment's operations include ethanol plants and marketing of ethanol and co-products.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates in various regions, with significant activities in the U.S., Mexico, and Canada. The report highlights the impact of geopolitical tensions and regulatory changes in these regions on operations.
  • Sales Units and Production Volumes: The company reported significant production volumes across its segments, with the Refining segment producing large quantities of gasolines and distillates, and the Renewable Diesel segment focusing on renewable diesel and SAF.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued strong demand for petroleum-based transportation fuels, although it faces challenges from regulatory changes and geopolitical tensions. The company is also evaluating strategic alternatives for its operations in California, including the planned cessation of refining operations at the Benicia Refinery by April 2026.
  • Operational Challenges: The company faced an asset impairment loss of $1.1 billion related to its California operations, impacting its financial results. This was due to increased operational restrictions and new reporting requirements in the state.
  • Capital Investments: Valero made $1.1 billion in capital investments during the first six months of 2025, focusing on sustaining and growing its operations across various segments.

SEC Filing: VALERO ENERGY CORP/TX [ VLO ] - 10-Q - Jul. 24, 2025

data.title: Cyclicals outshine defensives, hinting "not just yet" on next recession
data.description:
Key points:
  • S&P 500, Nasdaq modestly green; Dow dips
  • Tech leads S&P 500 sector gainers; Cons Disc down most
  • Euro STOXX 600 index up ~0.2%; ECB leaves rates unchanged
  • Dollar up; bitcoin gains ~1%; crude rises ~1.5%; gold dips
  • U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield edges up to ~4.40%

CYCLICALS OUTSHINE DEFENSIVES, HINTING "NOT JUST YET" ON NEXT RECESSION

Relative valuations across major themes can be highly informative of investor sentiment and economic expectations.

Scott Opsal, director of research and equities at the Leuthold Group, is noting unusually high valuations of cyclicals vs defensives are indicating a positive outlook on the business cycle and hinting at a risk-on mentality.

Here is a chart of the ratio of S&P 500 cyclical sectors, including industrials , materials and consumer discretionary vs S&P 500 defensive groups, including healthcare , staples and utilities . With cyclicals outperforming, the ratio hit fresh record highs in mid-July.

Periods when the reverse is true would reflect concerns of an economic slowdown and a desire to play it safe when it comes to equity risk exposure.

According to Opsal, cyclical valuations are elevated across the board, and this consensus indicates that economic concerns are at an ebb, and recession risk, and the contractionary impact on cyclical earnings, is rather subdued at this time.

However, he says consumer cyclicals (household durables, autos, and airlines) seem much weaker than the commercial and industrial groups, suggesting that confidence in business activity might be more robust than confidence in the consumer outlook.

"Defensives have pockets of weakness in both Health Care and Staples. The fact that it is not confined to one or two groups with idiosyncratic risks tells us that investors are not interested in battening down the hatches and protecting against a profits recession," writes Opsal in a note.

He adds that this low demand for safety can be seen in the recent performance of thematic ETFs such as the iShares MSCI USA min vol factor ETF , which he says has gained just 6% vs an S&P 500 index surge of more than 20% over the last three months or so.

Opsal's bottom line is that "whether one is a portfolio manager looking to play the momentum in cyclicals or take advantage of a relative value opportunity in defensives, it is worthwhile to keep an eye on this telling relationship. Likewise, those searching for clues on the timing of the next recession may consider this as one metric that is intimating 'not just yet.'"

(Terence Gabriel)

*****

EARLIER ON LIVE

data.title: Chevron tells Hess staff to focus on safety as they await job updates
data.description:
Chevron leaders told Hess employees to stay focused on safe operations and that they will hear next week if they will continue to have jobs following the oil producer's acquisition, according to two Hess employees who spoke with Reuters.

Chevron, the second-largest U.S. oil producer, closed its $55 billion acquisition of Hess on Friday after prevailing in a landmark legal fight against Exxon Mobil that delayed the closing by over a year.

The deal was critical to Chevron CEO Mike Wirth's strategy to improve the business as it sought to cut costs and faced investor concerns about future growth prospects.

The company will lay off 575 Hess employees in Houston effective September 26, according to a notice filed with the Texas Workforce Commission, or about 32% of staff based on Hess' headcount at the end of last year.

During town hall meetings at Hess' Houston office on Tuesday, Chevron representatives presented a slide that showed safety incidents have risen during volatile periods historically, cautioning them to continue prioritizing safety, according to the two employees, who declined to be named to discuss an internal meeting.

Hess staff were told they will be notified next week if they have a full-time position with the company moving forward or a short-term role to help with the transition.

"These are difficult decisions which we do not make lightly," a Chevron spokesperson said, adding the company will offer severance and other support.

The emphasis on safety comes after Wirth warned Chevron employees earlier this year that the company saw an increase in close calls that could have resulted in serious injuries or fatalities.

Chevron and Hess staff were asked not to contact each other until the integration is complete, according to the two Hess employees and an internal Chevron message that Reuters reviewed.

"We need them to focus on safe operations and on navigating the complex changes that result from this merger," said the internal Chevron message.

data.title: Michelin Backs Guidance Despite Earnings Drop — Update
data.description:
By Cristina Gallardo

Compagnie Generale des Etablissements Michelin backed its full-year guidance despite a drop in first-half earnings, partly caused by the threat of higher tariffs.

The French car-parts manufacturer said Thursday that sales fell to 13.03 billion euros ($15.34 billion) in the first half of the year, from 13.48 billion euros in the same period last year.

The group posted segment operating income--its preferred earnings metric--of 1.5 billion euros, down 18.5% from the prior year, and operating income of 1.2 billion euros, a 23.6% decrease.

Net income stood at 840 million euros, down 27.8%.

Consensus estimates provided by the company had seen sales at 13.08 billion euros, segment operating income of 1.5 billion euros, and net income of 1.01 billion euros.

Sales dropped 3.4% for the period, partly due to the strengthening of the euro, Michelin said. Tire demand remained broadly depressed, especially for truck, agricultural and infrastructure tires, it added.

Demand in Europe fell by 8% over the first half, as new vehicle sales were hit by the erosion of purchasing power, Michelin said.

The North and Central America markets contracted sharply because of the turbulence generated by the threat of higher tariffs, which weighed heavily on manufacturers' activity in the first half of 2025, Michelin said. In addition, the transition to electric or hybrid vehicles slowed during the period, it added.

In China, demand grew by 10% as consumers took advantage of Chinese government incentives for the purchase of new vehicles introduced last year, the group said.

Michelin reiterated its full-year guidance and said it expects sell-in tire markets to be stable compared with 2024, despite the uncertainty caused by customs tariffs and exchange rates.

Write to Cristina Gallardo at cristina.gallardo@wsj.com

data.title: EU wheat extends fall to one-week low
data.description:
European wheat prices extended the previous session's fall on Thursday as ample supplies, thin demand and a high euro continued to weigh on prices.

Benchmark September milling wheat (BL2U5) on Paris-based Euronext, was down 1.50% by 1602 GMT to 196.50 euros ($231.22) a metric ton.

The most-traded wheat contract on the Chicago Board of Trade was down 0.4% by the same time.

"European wheat cannot afford to rise if it wants to attract demand, especially with such a high euro," a trader said.

The euro was hovering near four-year highs against the dollar, making euro-denominated goods less competitive on the world market. The European Central Bank left interest rates steady at 2%, as expected, on Thursday.

Updates of an annual crop tour in North Dakota showed yields for U.S. spring wheat crops are likely to fall from last year's record per-acre output but remain above the five-year average, the Wheat Quality Council said on Wednesday.

The U.S. Department of Agriculture on Thursday incorrectly reported a sale of U.S. corn to China when the buyer was actually South Korea, the agency said.

The initial report had excited some grain traders because China is not a top buyer of U.S. corn and its demand for American agricultural goods has declined amid trade tensions between Washington and Beijing.

RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. For further information about how RNS and the London Stock Exchange use the personal data you provide us, please see our Privacy Policy.  END  POSUSANRVWUBUAR

data.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1137 ET - Vodafone Group's improving performance in Germany is likely to boost market confidence, Berenberg analysts write in a note. The German segment was the main positive in the company's results, with signs of recovery in service revenue, they say. Additionally, the U.K.-based telecom group reiterated its fiscal 2026 guidance, incorporating the recent merger with rival Three, which was completed last month. Overall, Vodafone delivered a marginally positive update, they add. Shares are up 3.6% at 86.18 pence. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

1106 ET - Vodafone Group's unchanged guidance has reassured investors, AJ Bell's Russ Mould writes in a note. The U.K.-based telecommunication group reaffirmed its fiscal 2026 targets for adjusted Ebitda after leases and adjusted free cash flow, supported by growth across all areas of the business except Germany, he notes. The region remains Vodafone's core market, and its continued struggles are a concern, he says. However, the company appears to be making progress in stemming loses there, he adds. Shares are up 4.1% at 86.56 pence. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

1046 ET - ITV continues to prove its resilience despite mounting competition, AJ Bell's Russ Mould writes in a note. While critics argue that ITV is becoming less relevant and facing pressure from giants like Netflix, the U.K. broadcaster is holding firm, he says. The company is demonstrating that it still has plenty to offer, he adds. ITV delivered a better-than-expected performance for the first half, supported by the strength of digital advertising, he notes. "The ITVX streaming platform has been a great success and the company is finding new ways to help advertisers reach a more targeted audience," he adds. Shares are up 13.2% at 87.75 pence.(najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0559 ET - RELX's performance looks solid in a set of first-half results marked by change in the company's reporting model that excludes print revenue from underlying growth, UBS analysts says in a research note. The U.K. information and analytics group reported first-half underlying revenue growth of 7% excluding print, which fell 21% and now represents 4% of the company's revenue, UBS says. "We believe RELX  is on a path to deliver 8% underlying growth--potentially by 2026--as now defined, alongside continuous margin expansion and large buybacks," the analysts say. RELX's adjusted operating profit was in line with consensus expectations, but its margin was ahead, UBS says. Shares trade 0.7% lower. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0437 ET - Vodafone Group's first-quarter results stand out for the lack of significant negatives, Barclays analysts write in a note. The U.K. telecommunication group delivered service revenue ahead of consensus in Germany, Europe, and at the group level, they note. Germany--which has been absorbing the impact from a regulatory change in the country--is slowly recovering, they say. However, the group remains behind its European peers, they add. Shares are up 2% at 84.84 pence. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0357 ET - STMicroelectronics' second-quarter results signal that the recovery in demand for some semiconductors is being hindered by tariff-related uncertainty and weakness in the automotive market, Citi analysts write in a note to clients. The European chip maker said demand for chips in personal electronics and industrial equipment had improved in the second quarter, but demand from automotive clients was below expectations. Chief Executive Jean-Marc Chery said that while overall sales should improve in the third quarter from the second quarter, the company was still operating in an uncertain environment. STMicroelectronics shares trade 12% lower at 23.67 euros. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

0353 ET - Nokia's third-quarter guidance for sequentially flat sales and stable operating margin implies a heavily-weighted fourth quarter, JPMorgan analysts Sandeep Deshpande and Craig A McDowell write. The guidance implies a 6% cut to third-quarter revenue and a 42% trim to operating-profit estimates, the bank says. Despite bright spots in the report, including strong order growth with cloud-infrastructure providers, second-quarter results show signs of exposure to weaker markets and operational challenges. JPMorgan sees little in the full report to support the shares, given the heavily weighted fourth quarter performance. "For 2025, the mid-point of the lowered operating profit guidance is 10.6% below Infront consensus expectations - we would expect consensus to move down by at least this level." Shares rise 0.3%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0349 ET - Infosys is likely to see steady earnings growth, based on its FY 2026 revised guidance, Axis Securities analysts say in a research report. The Indian digital services and consulting company slightly adjusted its FY 2026 revenue growth guidance to 1%-3% in constant currency terms from its previous guidance of 0%-3%, the analysts note. The company doesn't foresee any significant changes in 2Q FY 2026 and expects performance to stay at similar levels, which suggests continued caution amid a challenging macroeconomic environment. Moreover, management expects 2H FY 2026 to be weaker than 1H FY 2026 owing to normal seasonality. The brokerage resumes its coverage of the stock with a hold rating and a target price of INR1,680.00. Shares are 1.4% lower at INR1,551.70. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0058 ET - MediaTek's earnings are likely to gain from expanded customer reach in the artificial-intelligence application specific integrated circuit market, Daiwa Capital Markets analysts say. The company appears to be on track to ramp up its first AI ASIC project for a U.S.-based cloud service provider. The project is expected to generate US$1 billion revenue in 2026, the analysts say in a note. The Taiwanese chip designer is adding one more U.S.-based CSP to its AI ASIC portfolio, which could triple this business revenue to US$3 billion by 2027. The brokerage upgrades the stock's rating to buy from outperform and raises the target price to NT$1,666.00 from NT$1,550.00. Shares are unchanged at NT$1,430.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0051 ET - The worst is likely over for ASMPT, Citi analysts say in a research note. The semiconductor company has delivered a large thermocompression-bonding tool order to a leading memory chip maker, they say, while another client has begun using ASMPT's bonding tool for low-volume production of 12-layer HBM4 chips. Order volume could become more meaningful in 2026 and 2027 as next-generation AI chips ramp up, they say. Mainstream semiconductor outlook is also improving, driven by AI data centers and EV demand in China, the analysts say. Citi says that new orders for ASMPT's thermocompression-bonding tool could be positive catalysts for its share price. Citi maintains a buy call on ASMPT and raises its target price to HK$75.00 from HK$65.00. Shares are last at HK$69.00. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

2336 ET - WiseTech Global investors shouldn't be surprised if the logistics-software provider's annual result announcement is met by a big share-price move, according to Morgan Stanley analysts. They point out in a note that the stock typically moves by an average 15% in either direction on a result announcement, and warn that there are a larger than normal number of moving parts at the company this year. They reckon there is a 50% chance that WiseTech's annual result and fiscal 2026 guidance meet expectations. They see a 30% chance of stronger-than-anticipated guidance, which could push shares up by 10-20%. A miss in the other direction could mean a 10-20% share-price fall, they add. MS has an overweight rating and A$140.00 target price on the stock, which is down 0.3% at A$119.69. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

2331 ET - Tencent Holdings has ample room for AI monetization, CGS International analysts say in a research note. Chinese AI and cloud players, like Tencent, will be able to expand their capex plans with Nvidia resuming sales of H20 chips in China. Given WeChat's 1.4 billion users, "we believe Tencent has the most effective ecosystem for AI monetization," they say. The gap between profit and revenue growth will likely narrow in the coming quarters as Tencent ramps up AI spending. CGS International maintains a buy call on Tencent and keeps its target price at HK$654.00 on its growth potential across all business units empowered by AI technology over the next 3-5 years. Shares are last at HK$555.50. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Health Care Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Health Care sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0251 ET - Roche's keeping its 2025 guidance looks reassuring, Vontobel analyst Sibylle Bischofberger writes in a research note. The Swiss drugmaker booked revenue in line with analysts' expectations for the first half, and said it still sees sales growth in the mid-single digits for the year as a whole. First-half performance is "well within" that guidance, Bischofberger says. "Importantly, despite the current uncertain political environment from the U.S., Roche maintains its 2025 guidance," she says. Vontobel keeps a 303 Swiss francs price target and a buy rating on Roche stock. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

2359 ET - IHH Healthcare's integration of its India units, Fortis and Gleneagles Healthcare India, may enhance operational efficiency and drive long-term growth, says MBSB Research in a note. Fortis will manage five of the six hospitals within the GHI network. By integrating GHI's operations, Fortis can leverage its extensive network to improve procurement, implement better protocols, and provide consistent patient care, it says. The integration is expected to strengthen IHH's presence in India and unlock growth potential through operational synergies. Despite potential risks such as cultural differences and regulatory challenges, IHH appears well-positioned to manage them, it says. MBSB has a buy rating and target price of MYR8.18 on the stock, which is 0.3% higher at MYR6.64. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1830 ET - Healthcare providers across the board are feeling the weight of growing medical costs. Molina Healthcare trimmed its full-year guidance again, just weeks after an earlier cut, in part reflecting a recent acceleration in medical-cost trends. Other providers like Elevance Health and United Healthcare have similarly slashed full-year projections in recent months, while Centene pulled its guidance earlier this month. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1823 ET - The EPA is moving closer to approving the popular pesticide dicamba for farmer use in 2026. The agency opened a 30-day comment period for the proposed approval of three Dicamba-based products, including ones made by crop chemical giants Bayer and Syngenta. Dicamba is widely used among cotton and soybean farmers in the southeast who battle tough, glyphosate resistant weeds. A federal court in Arizona in 2024 vacated the EPA's registration of the chemical. The court said that the EPA's restrictions on the herbicides were not enough to prevent them from drifting to neighboring farmers' fields and damaging crops that lack genetically engineered resistance to the chemical. As a result, farmers weren't able to apply the pesticide this year. (patrick.thomas@wsj.com)

data.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1019 ET - The Los Angeles wildfires in January impacted $51.7 billion worth of homes--or roughly 11,000 residential properties--according to Redfin. The report analyzes data from the Los Angeles City Council. The council shared a list of 11,125 residential parcels in the city of Los Angeles that were inspected by the Los Angeles Department of Building and Safety after the wildfires. Many, but not all, were completely destroyed by the fires. Most homes in this analysis were impacted by the Palisades Fire. This analysis excludes the impact of the Eaton Fire, which impacted the Los Angeles suburb of Altadena, meaning the total value of homes destroyed in the January fires likely far exceeds $51.7 billion. Redfin says the typical impacted home had a value of $3.7 million prior to the fires. Nearly 100 properties had a value of over $20 million prior to the fires. (chris.wack@wsj.com)

0823 ET - The collapse of UniCredit's takeover offer for Italian peer Banco BPM calls into question how likely many of these transactions will face obstacles as they progress through the execution phase, Mergermarket's Lucinda Guthrie says. The deal was the fourth largest ongoing listed transaction in Europe, after Monte dei Paschi's bid for Mediobanca, the Covestro-Adnoc deal and BBVA's pursuit of Sabadell. The move highlights the complexity of M&A for European banks, the head of the M&A data and intelligence platform says. "Despite this, dealmakers are expecting an active pipeline of up to 50 active situations in the banking space in 2H in Europe, spurred by a softer interest rate environment, the need to digitalise and a fear of missing out as deals materialise in the space," she says. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0349 ET - Infosys is likely to see steady earnings growth, based on its FY 2026 revised guidance, Axis Securities analysts say in a research report. The Indian digital services and consulting company slightly adjusted its FY 2026 revenue growth guidance to 1%-3% in constant currency terms from its previous guidance of 0%-3%, the analysts note. The company doesn't foresee any significant changes in 2Q FY 2026 and expects performance to stay at similar levels, which suggests continued caution amid a challenging macroeconomic environment. Moreover, management expects 2H FY 2026 to be weaker than 1H FY 2026 owing to normal seasonality. The brokerage resumes its coverage of the stock with a hold rating and a target price of INR1,680.00. Shares are 1.4% lower at INR1,551.70. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0317 ET - Lloyds Banking Group's underlying performance looks strong as impairments continue to fuel better-than-projected profit, default rates remain low and borrowers remain resilient, Hargreaves Lansdown's Matt Britzman says. "The [market] reaction might be a little muted, though, given the lack of guidance upgrade off the back of a good set of numbers," he writes in a note. The British bank is exposed to a regulatory probe into car loans given its significant share of the market. The Supreme Court is set to make a judgement on this case this month. The fact that the group hasn't taken further provisions for this could be seen as a sign of confidence, he adds. Shares are up 0.5% at 78 pence. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0255 ET - BNP Paribas's solid print was supported by good cost control and strong revenue from its global markets division, JPMorgan says in a research note after the French bank's second-quarter print. Most metrics came in ahead of consensus expectations, analysts write. "France, Belgium and Personal Finance performed well whilst Italy, Arval and Asset Management were a bit more mixed," they note. JPM still sees the lender's targets for this year and next as ambitious and would expect limited earnings per share upgrades at this stage. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0252 ET - Deutsche Bank's pretax profit beat doesn't reflect the true picture, Keefe, Bruyette & Woods say in a research note after the German bank's second-quarter results. "Low quality revenue beats in [corporate and other] and underwhelming pre-provision performances in Corporate and Private Bank will likely lead to minimal upgrades (if any)," analysts write. The results fail to excite, especially given the recent share-price gains, they add. Its capital ratio and communication around the impact of capital requirement regulation 3--a EU regulation to strengthen financial stability among credit institutions and investment firms--are positive, they note. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0253 ET - Sabadell's new targets are achievable and not overly ambitious, balancing ambition and realism at a key time in the hostile takeover bid by BBVA, RBC's Pablo de la Torre Cuevas says in a note. The Spanish bank raised its target on 2025 return on tangible equity, a profitability measure, to 14.5% from around 14% previously. It is aiming for 16% in 2027 compared with latest consensus of around 14.5%, the analyst says. The bank's refreshed focus on Spain should benefit from the recovery in lending in the country, with continued resilient earnings even amid falling interest rates in Europe. The biggest gap between current consensus and Sabadell's new targets appears to be in total cost of risk as well as in overall balance sheet growth, he adds. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

0252 ET - Sabadell's ambitious new plan alongside a decent earnings beat should be well received by the market, KBW analysts Hugo Cruz and Ben Maher say in a note. The Spanish lender targets return on tangible equity of 16% in 2027 and total returns equivalent to more than 40% of market cap. However, this is a plan that requires growth, though that makes sense given Sabadell's positioning in Spain, the analysts say. The bank also issued better RoTE guidance for 2025 on the back of better operating expenses and cost of risk. Meanwhile, second-quarter net profit of 486 million euros beat consensus expectations by 11% on lower fees, impairments and taxes, they add. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

2305 ET - Macquarie management's confidence in the Australian financial giant's performance encourages UBS analysts, who say its 1Q update is slightly softer than the market was expecting. The UBS analysts tell clients in a note that the update implies a 10% fall in 1Q profit. However, management says the performance is in line with their expectations and influenced by the timing of asset realizations. This offers some reassurance to the UBS analysts, who are starting to see Macquarie deliver a series of misses relative to expectations. Its reliance on commodities and global markets reduces earnings visibility, they warn. UBS has a neutral rating and A$225.00 target price on the stock, which is down 4.4% at A$215.37. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

2131 ET - CIMB Group's 2Q results are expected to show stable net interest margins and strong non-interest income, supported by trading and forex income, Maybank IB analyst Desmond Ch'ng says in a note. Asset quality may remain steady, with credit costs anticipated to be benign, he reckons. However, loan growth could be a weak point, as the lender remains cautious on lending to the corporate and commercial sectors due to competitive yields. Ch'ng assumes CIMB's 2025 loan growth to be 4.5%, compared with management's 5%-7% target. Maybank maintains a hold rating on CIMB and keeps target price at MYR7.60. Shares are 0.3% higher at MYR6.69. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1830 ET - Healthcare providers across the board are feeling the weight of growing medical costs. Molina Healthcare trimmed its full-year guidance again, just weeks after an earlier cut, in part reflecting a recent acceleration in medical-cost trends. Other providers like Elevance Health and United Healthcare have similarly slashed full-year projections in recent months, while Centene pulled its guidance earlier this month. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1348 ET - PulteGroup is showing impressive resilience in a tough macroeconomic environment, BofA Securities analysts say in a research note. The Atlanta home builder logged a smaller profit and lower revenue in 2Q, but the figures came in ahead of Wall Street's estimates. The analysts note that gross margins guidance for 3Q and 4Q looks better than investors' expectations and add that they expect mix to be a margin tailwind in 2026 as it delivers more higher margin active adult homes from recently opened communities. "PulteGroup has attractive ROE & capital return relative to other builders and we expect resilient order/margin performance in a tough housing backdrop," say the analysts, who increase their price objective to $140 from $127. Shares tick down 1% after rallying 11% Tuesday on 2Q results. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0957 ET - Oil futures gain ground with signs of U.S. progress toward trade deals taking some of the tariff-related pessimism out of the market. Yesterday's U.S. inventory data showing a 3.2 million barrel crude stock draw is also supportive of prices, Dennis Kissler of BOK Financial says in a note. Technically September WTI "remains in a sideways bullish type of structure," he says, with major resistance at $66.11 and support at $64.22. WTI is up 0.9% at $65.85 a barrel and Brent is 0.7% higher at $69.01. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0640 ET - While Centrica posted in-line results and reiterated its guidance, consensus estimates have yet to appropriately incorporate the U.K. energy company's sale of the Cygnus gas field, JPMorgan analysts say in a note. While the consensus for earnings per share of 12.6 pence appears to be consistent with company guidance, JPM continues to expect 11.8 pence earnings per share. Centrica swinging to an overall loss highlights the challenging backdrop of the country's energy sector, which has read-across for peers operating in the U.K., such as E.ON and RWE, they say. Shares trade 1.6% higher at 161.25 pence. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

0521 ET - Repsol delivered a surprisingly strong beat to consensus expectations, RBC Capital Markets analysts Biraj Borkhataria and Adnan Dhanani write. Madrid-listed energy company's second-quarter net income of 702 million euros was well ahead of consensus expectations of 499 million euros. The beat was primarily driven by the upstream division, they write. The operating environment also looks supporting going into the third quarter, with refining margins potentially delivering a boost to earnings, they add. Meanwhile, Repsol should be a beneficiary from any short-term outages during the U.S. hurricane season, they write. Outages in the U.S. would limit supply and deliver a boost to margins. Shares trade up 1.4% at 13.385 euros. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0400 ET - TotalEnergies' second-quarter earnings are in line with expectations, Jefferies analysts Giacomo Romeo and Kai Ye Loh write. Cash flow from operations pre-working capital is 5% above expectations, they write. Overall, the sustainability of its $2 billion quarterly buyback will be in focus when the management takes questions from investors later Thursday. Shares trade down 1.4% at 52.58 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0312 ET - TotalEnergies' rising net debt will begin to weigh on investor sentiment, RBC Capital Markets analysts Biraj Borkhataria and Adnan Dhanani write. Debt rose significantly during the quarter due to a combination of a large capital expenditure plan, $2 billion of net acquisitions and a further working capital build, they write. The French energy company will need to reassure investors that debt will begin to fall in the second half, they write. Investors will not reward buybacks that are paid out of the balance sheet indefinitely, they add. Shares fall 1.7% to 52.41 euros in opening trade. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

2349 ET - Citi analyst Paul McTaggart isn't sure how much of Woodside Energy's stronger-than-expected 2Q cost performance can be repeated. He tells clients in a note that it isn't clear how much of the energy producer's lower cost base is due to structural changes, and how much relates to one-off factors. More positively, he points out that Woodside effectively upgraded its 2025 production guidance by 3% given the impact of the Greater Angostura divestment. Citi stays neutral on the stock while it waits on further news on Woodside's efforts to sell down its stake in its Louisiana LNG gas-export infrastructure. It raises the target price by 19% to A$25.50. Shares are up 1.1% to A$25.48. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

2245 ET - Petronas Dagangan could experience a mild positive impact from the government's reduction of RON95 fuel prices to 1.99 ringgit a liter from 2.05 ringgit a liter by end-September, CIMB Securities' Nurzulaikha Azali says in a note. The price cut may support incremental demand amid generally inelastic fuel consumption in Malaysia, and retail volumes will likely remain stable, she says. The earnings impact on the gas-station operator is expected to be neutral, as margins are protected under the automatic pricing mechanism, whereby the government reimburses the difference between market and subsidized prices. However, delays in subsidy reimbursements could strain working capital, the analyst adds. CIMB maintains its hold rating and MYR20.90 target price on the stock, which is unchanged at MYR21.00. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2217 ET - Malaysia's recent fiscal support measures, including cash handouts and fuel price cuts, could boost consumer sentiment and support near-term spending, say CIMB Securities analysts Ivy Ng Lee Fang and Lim Yue Jia. The auto, consumer, and REIT sectors, along with Petronas Dagangan, could benefit from increased sales in 2H, they say in a note. However, the analysts don't expect a significant impact on overall KLCI earnings, as the key beneficiaries aren't index heavyweights. CIMB maintains its end-2025 KLCI target at 1560, with 99 Speedmart, Mynews Holdings, Fraser & Neave, and Mr. D.I.Y Group (M) among the top picks in the consumer sector. The KLCI is up 0.1% at 1530.70. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2144 ET - Ampol's performance in its non-refining businesses looks impressive to Macquarie analysts given the challenges it faces from weak fuel volumes and tobacco sales. They write in a note that the Australian company's ability to grow convenience retail Ebit shows discipline in fuel pricing and highlights its premium fuel strategies. They raise their EPS forecast for fiscal 2025 on lower interest and tax expenses, but cut by 3.6% for fiscal 2026 and by 3.2% for fiscal 2027 on expected lower fuel sales volumes. Macquarie keeps a neutral rating and A$27.50 target price on the stock, which is up 0.3% at A$27.85. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

2006 ET - Oil rises in the early Asian session amid optimism toward U.S.-EU trade talks. The U.S. and the EU are working toward a deal that would set a 15% tariff for most EU goods to the U.S., say ANZ Research analysts, noting media reports. This follows a similar U.S. deal with Japan, they say in a research report. The prospect of a U.S.-EU trade deal has sparked a risk-on tone and boosted sentiment, the analysts add. Front-month WTI crude oil futures are up 0.4% at $65.50/bbl; front-month Brent crude oil futures are 0.3% higher at $68.74/bbl. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

1653 ET - U.S. oil-and-gas producers announced deals with a total value of $30.5 billion during this year's first half, a 60% drop from 1H 2024, according to Enverus. The decline reflects a slowdown in the fast consolidation that the U.S. shale industry experienced in the past few years, as energy companies fret about "volatility in commodity and equity markets," says Andrew Dittmar, a principal analyst at the energy-focused data analytics company. "That added an additional barrier to a market that was already challenged by the lack of remaining attractive opportunities for public [energy companies], especially in the Permian Basin." Two transactions drove up this year's total deal value: EOG's acquisition of Encino for $5.6 billion and the $4.06 billion merger between Viper Energy Partners and Sitio Royalties, both of which invest in mineral and royalty rights. (luis.garcia@wsj.com; @lhvgarcia)

1524 ET - Crude oil futures pull up from earlier lows, but not enough to avoid a fourth consecutive session of losses with prices consolidating as the market awaits more clarity on the outcome of U.S. tariffs and sanctions against Russia. WTI was supported by the EIA's report of a 3.2 million barrel crude oil inventory draw for last week, while a second large weekly build in diesel stocks eased some of the concerns about low supplies of the fuel. WTI settles down 0.1% at $65.25 as September takes the front month position. Brent slips 0.1% to $68.51 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0357 ET - Anglo American's key division reported a solid second-quarter production results, RBC Capital Markets analysts write. It is a challenging reporting period for the miner given the extensive restructuring that is taking place. However, the good news is that production and unit cost guidance for the businesses that it will keep remain unchanged, the analysts write. Copper production was in line with the analysts' expectations while diamond production and refined PGM production was below. Steelmaking coal production of 2.1 million metric tons was 27% ahead of expectations, they write. Shares trade up 0.2% at 2,352 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

2351 ET - Petronas Chemicals' 2Q earnings could be hurt by operational issues, weak product prices and global oversupply, CIMB Securities analyst Nurzulaikha Azali says in a note. Earnings are projected to decline 5% sequentially in 2Q, weighed by ongoing operational issues at its plants, as well as continued downtime at the Pengerang Petrochemicals Complex, she says. Challenges persist with declining prices in key segments like olefins and fertilisers, she says. The ramp-up of new capacities, especially in Southeast Asia, is expected to keep pressuring prices lower, she reckons. The overall outlook remains cautious with limited near-term earnings upside, due to structural issues and continued market weakness, she adds. CIMB maintains a hold rating on Petronas Chemicals, with an unchanged target price of MYR3.53. Shares are 0.3% lower at MYR3.36.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2228 ET - Iron ore prices are lower in early Asian trade amid signs of stronger supply. Vale, one of the world's biggest producers, said it produced 83.6 million tons of iron ore in 2Q, the highest 2Q production since 2021. Still, sentiment is broadly positive this week, following the announcement that China will be proceeding with the 1.2 trillion yuan hydropower project, ANZ Research analysts say in a research note. That could provide a massive boost to the concrete and steel sectors, which have been struggling amid a slowdown in the property sector, they add. The most actively traded September iron-ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is 0.7% lower at CNY809.5 a ton. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

2222 ET - Lynas Rare Earths' 4Q production, sales and prices are better than expected, according to Jefferies analyst Mitch Ryan. That is partially offset by disappointments on unit costs and capital expenditure, he says. "Lynas will need to sign strategic, new offtake mechanisms or partnerships to justify current valuations," Ryan says. It "is strategically well positioned to do so, capturing any market strength through production expansions and diversification." Jefferies has a buy rating and A$10.00 target on Lynas. The stock is up 3.0% at A$10.44. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

1935 ET - Investors are likely to react positively to Fortescue's strong hematite operational performance and a reduction in energy spending, Citi analyst Paul McTaggart says in a note. Fortescue's FY 2025 iron-ore shipments are a record and 4% higher year over year, while hematite costs edged lower, he notes. Energy spending in FY 2026 is reduced to roughly US$700 million versus about US$1.1 billion in FY 2025. Realized 4Q pricing for Fortescue's hematite product is broadly as expected, McTaggart adds. Citi has a neutral rating and A$16.00 target on Fortescue. The stock ended Wednesday at A$18.21. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

1432 ET - Freeport-McMoRan expects a benefit to its annual U.S. sales of about $1.7 billion thanks to the U.S. premium over LME copper prices following President Trump's pledge to slap a 50% tariff on imports of the metal. Freeport CEO Kathleen Quirk says on a call with analysts that this estimate was based on a premium of approximately $1.25 a pound, or about 28% above the LME's closing price Tuesday. She says the U.S. imports about half of its copper cathodes, with her company producing the majority of the rest. Freeport-McMoRan is off 2.3% at $44.75. (mark.long@wsj.com)

1423 ET - Front-month gold futures snap a three-day winning streak by dropping 1.3% to $3,394.10 a troy ounce. A lift in the U.S. dollar pressured gold following the U.S./Japan trade agreement. "The deal …was seen as a positive step toward de-escalating trade tensions in Asia and contributed to a mild unwinding of safe-haven positioning," says Quasar Elizundia of Pepperstone in a note. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1602 GMT - Tesla's long-term upside is largely predicated on its plans for lower cost cars, autonomous vehicles and AI products. But it offered few updates to these critical releases with its 2Q results, Truist analysts say. "At a minimum, considering they are already supposedly in production, we expected the company to describe aspects of its new lower-cost models (cost, features, estimated production, etc.) Alas, management offered no such details," the analysts say, adding that the company also said little about its autonomous vehicles, Optimus robot or Dojo supercomputer. Tesla also didn't provide any meaningful future financial guidance, the analysts say. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1549 GMT - To fund its long-term bets in AI and autonomous vehicles, Tesla is going to need cash. But the company only generated $146 million in free cash flow in 2Q, down from $1.34 billion the previous year. That could be a warning sign for its future cash reserves, say Truist analysts in a research note. "Considering the turbulence from tariffs, the reduction in consumer & regulatory credits, and ongoing capex needs, management acknowledged that the next few quarters could get worse, and potentially drain some of the company's $37b cash position," the analysts say. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1546 GMT - Tesla's EV sales are falling, but that shouldn't matter much if it hits its goals for autonomous vehicles, which it wants to make available to half the U.S. population by year-end. Wedbush analysts estimate autonomous vehicles could be a $1 trillion opportunity for Tesla and that its laser-focus on AI will reward investors who stick with the company through the EV struggles. "Musk now entering the picture as a wartime CEO to put TSLA on an aggressive AI-focused strategy represents the biggest and best possible news for Tesla investors," the analysts say. Wedbush maintains its outperform rating and $500 price target. Shares drop 9.1% to $302.29. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1243 GMT - American Airlines shares are under pressure after the company offered a dim outlook for the current quarter. The company plans for capacity to be up 2% to 3%, which isn't far from the consensus view for a 3% gain, TD Cowen analysts say in a research note. But it also says revenue could rise as much as 1% or drop as much as 2%. The consensus view was that revenue would rise up to half a percentage point, the analysts say. The revenue outlook implies that revenue per available seat mile, a metric for capacity utilization, will be down 3%, they say. American also expects to post a 3Q adjusted loss of 10 cents to 60 cents a share. The analyst consensus was for a tiny 1-cent adjusted loss, according to TD Cowen. The analysts point out that American has consistently outperformed its guidance after changing up its leadership last year. Shares slide 6%. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1004 GMT - China's upcoming Politburo meeting could release a consolidated plan to reduce excess competition in key industries, Minsheng Securities analysts say in a note. The campaign will likely target EV and solar industries and focus on regulating behaviors of both local governments and companies, they say. The meeting is expected to show the progress of the government's economic plan from 2026 to 2030. While stimulus plans are unlikely to be imminent, as 1H growth was steady, the meeting may provide clues on potential incremental policy measures, especially for property and consumption sectors, they add. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0932 GMT - JPMorgan expects Kuehne + Nagel to see a downward revision to consensus estimates given that the 2025 EBIT guidance has been lowered around 5% at midpoint, analysts say in a research note. The Swiss logistics company posted second-quarter earnings before interest and taxes of 342 million Swiss francs, coming in 6% below the company-compiled consensus. As the guidance downgrade was broadly expected, volume growth provides some supportive second-quarter comparatives for peers, the analysts say. Overall, the analysts say they continue to view the backdrop as challenging, with consensus estimates under risk. Shares trade 0.5% higher at 173.95 Swiss francs. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

0916 GMT - Wizz Air's engine issues remain but are improving, Davy Research's Stephen Furlong and Ava Costello say. The London-listed carrier had to ground 41 aircraft at the end of its first quarter for inspections, versus 46 aircraft parked at the same time last year, they say. The group estimates it will ground about 35 jets in 2026, an improvement on the 44 in 2025, they add. An agreement signed with Pratt & Whitney will give Wizz Air access to additional engine spare parts. This will help to speed up the return to air of the grounded fleet, now expected in 2027, the analysts say. Shares are down 0.3% at 10.80 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0854 GMT - Wizz Air's yield and cost recovery should be regarded with caution, Jefferies analysts say after the London-listed carrier posted a miss on first-quarter sales and Ebitda. The company didn't give full-year guidance, Jefferies notes. Banking the miss on Ebitda is a 3% cut to full-year consensus, they note. The company's exit from Abu Dhabi can exacerbate cost pressures, they add, warning the group faces the risks of consensus cuts. Shares are down 1.75% at 10.64 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0837 GMT - Wizz Air's slightly worse second quarter and uncertainty on growth beyond summer could lead to cuts to the London-listed carrier's consensus estimates, J.P. Morgan's Harry Gowers and Bhavpreet Batra say. Wizz Air is looking to slow growth and redesign the network, but there are several unknowns around discussions with partners on the fleet trajectory and its exit from Abu Dhabi, the analysts say. It may take time to convince the market of the changes, they add. Shares are down 1.85% at 10.63 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0833 GMT - Wizz Air faces near-term challenges and negative earnings momentum, RBC Europe's Ruairi Cullinane and Jakub Glinkowski say. The London-listed carrier's recovery prospects on a 12-month view are limited, they say. Other European airlines offer a more attractive risk-reward profile, they add. Wizz Air could recover in the medium-term, from 2027, due to a reduction in grounded fleet and some strategic steps, they say. Shares are down 0.2% at 10.81 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0822 GMT - Wizz Air's first-quarter net income of 38.4 million euros failed to meet consensus forecasts of 78.1 million euros, and the London-listed carrier has struggled to boost capacity, Peel Hunt analysts Alexander Paterson and Ivor Jones say. The cost per available seat kilometer--a metric that indicates how much it costs an airline to fly one seat for one kilometer--was also higher due to more expensive fuel and airport charges, they note. The company's guidance suggests a lower load factor and higher CASK for the second quarter than Peel Hunt's estimates, they say. Shares are down 0.5% at 10.78 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0814 GMT - Tesla shares slide premarket after Elon Musk's electric-vehicle maker posted lower net income for the second quarter. Tesla stock closed 0.1% higher at $332.56 on Wednesday ahead of results. Shares are down 6.3% at $311.75 in premarket trading Thursday. The company booked $1.17 billion in net income for the quarter, down from $1.4 billion the year prior. It also logged $22.5 billion in revenue, down 12% on year. Revenue from its automotive business fell 16%. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

data.title: Teck says discussions ongoing over Chile mines
data.description:
Teck Resources said on Thursday that discussions around potential opportunities at its Quebrada Blanca (QB) and Collahuasi copper operations in Chile are ongoing.

Collahuasi, a large copper mine, is operated by Glencore , Anglo American and Japan Collahuasi Resources.

data.title: Lost Money on Biohaven Ltd. (BHVN)? Contact Levi & Korsinsky Before September 12, 2025 to Join Class Action
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / July 24, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Biohaven Ltd. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/biohaven-ltd-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=158003&wire=1&utm_campaign=12

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Biohaven Ltd. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between March 24, 2023 and May 14, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (i) The company's product candidate, troriluzole's regulatory prospects as a treatment for SCA, and/or the sufficiency of data that Biohaven submitted in support of troriluzole's regulatory approval for this indication, were overstated; (ii) BHV-7000's efficacy and clinical prospects as a treatment for bipolar disorder were likewise overstated; (iii) all the foregoing, once revealed, was likely to have a significant negative impact on Biohaven's business and financial condition; and (iv) as a result, defendants' public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Biohaven Ltd. stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/biohaven-ltd-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=158003&wire=1&utm_campaign=12 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

33 Whitehall Street, 17th Floor

New York, NY 10004

jlevi@levikorsinsky.com

Tel: (212) 363-7500

https://zlk.com/
